text:
Nationalist China: Chiang Ching-kuo, Nationalist China's security chief and the son of Chiang Kai-shek, acknowledged that three plane loads of supplies, including small arms and ammunition, were air-dropped to the Chinese Nationalist irregular forces in northern Burma during November. Chiang, who denied that the irregulars were planning an offensive, said the air-drop was mainly for morale purposes.

Air-drops to the Nationalist irregulars have previously been reported. About 20 officers were also sent to northern Burma recently to supervise the utilization of arms and to give political indoctrination. An additional resupply operation, to include heavier arms, is scheduled for air-drop to the irregulars in February
